definitions.definition: Infrastructure (such as a web server) designed to attract attackers in order to learn how better to defend systems.

definitions.source_name: INCIBE Glosario de terminos de ciberseguridad
definitions.definition:
Herramienta de seguridad instalada en una red o sistema informático que permite, ante un ataque informático por parte de terceros, poder detectarlo y obtener información tanto del ataque como del atacante.

Sinónimo: Señuelo

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A system (e.g., a web server) or system resource (e.g., a file on a server) that is designed to be attractive to potential crackers and intruders, like honey is attractive to bears.

definitions.source_name: Cyber Law Toolkit Glossary
definitions.definition: A deception technique in which a person seeking to defend computer systems against malicious cyber operations uses a physical or virtual environment designed to lure the attention of intruders.

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: An evaluation methodology whereby assessors search for vulnerabilities and attempt to circumvent the security features of a network and/or information system.

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: An authorised test of a computer network or system, designed to look for security weaknesses with the end aim of fixing them.

definitions.source_name: Texas DIR Cybersecurity Terminology
definitions.definition: Security testing in which evaluators mimic real-world attacks in an attempt to identify ways to circumvent the security features of an application, system, or network. Penetration testing often involves issuing real attacks on real systems and data, using the same tools and techniques used by actual attackers. Most penetration tests involve looking for combinations of vulnerabilities on a single system or multiple systems that can be used to gain more access than could be achieved through a single vulnerability.

definitions.source_name: NY State ITS Glossary
definitions.definition: Test of the overall strength of an SE’s defenses (technology, processes, people) by simulating the objectives and actions of an attacker.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Testing used in vulnerability analysis for vulnerability assessment, trying to reveal vulnerabilities of the system based on the information about the system gathered during the relevant evaluation activities.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A method of testing where testers target individual binary components or the application as a whole to determine whether intra or intercomponent vulnerabilities can be exploited to compromise the application, its data, or its environment resources.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A test methodology in which assessors, typically working under specific constraints, attempt to circumvent or defeat the security features of a system.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A test methodology in which assessors, typically working under specific constraints, attempt to circumvent or defeat the security features of an information system.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A test methodology in which assessors, using all available documentation (e.g., system design, source code, manuals) and working under specific constraints, attempt to circumvent the security features of an information system.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Security testing in which evaluators mimic real-world attacks in an attempt to identify ways to circumvent the security features of an application, system, or network. Penetration testing often involves issuing real attacks on real systems and data, using the same tools and techniques used by actual attackers. Most penetration tests involve looking for combinations of vulnerabilities on a single system or multiple systems that can be used to gain more access than could be achieved through a single vulnerability.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Testing that verifies the extent to which a system, device or process resists active attempts to compromise its security.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A test methodology intended to circumvent the security function of a system. Note: Penetration testing may leverage system documentation (e.g., system design, source code, manuals) and is conducted within specific constraints. Some penetration test methods use brute force techniques.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A test methodology intended to circumvent the security function of a system. Note: Penetration testing may leverage system documentation (e.g., system design, source code, manuals) and is conducted within specific constraints. Some penetration test methods use brute force techniques.

definitions.source_name: COV IT Glossary (Virginia)
definitions.definition: A penetration test is a method of evaluating the security computer system or network simulating an attack by a malicious user.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
1. (O) /noun/ Synonym for "plain text".

2. (I) /adjective/ Referring to plain text. Usage: Commonly used
instead of "plain-text". (Compare: ciphertext, cleartext.)

3. (D) /noun/ Synonym for "cleartext".

Deprecated Definition: IDOCs SHOULD NOT use this term as a synonym
for "cleartext". Cleartext data is, by definition, not encrypted;
but plaintext data that is input to an encryption operation may be

cleartext data or may be ciphertext data that was output from a
previous encryption operation. (See: superencryption.)

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: Unencrypted information.

definitions.source_name: AWS Glossary Reference
definitions.definition: Information that has not been encrypted , as opposed to ciphertext .

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: Unencrypted information.

definitions.source_name: CWE Glossary (also CAPEC, CAR)
definitions.definition: information that is used as the input to an encryption algorithm, which might contain already-encrypted text. Many people use the "plaintext" term to mean "unencrypted," and others may use "cleartext" to mean the same thing.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Intelligible data that has meaning and can be understood without the application of cryptography.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Intelligible data that has meaning and can be understood without the application of decryption.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Unencrypted information that may be input to an encryption operation. Note: Plain text is not a synonym for clear text. See clear text.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Information that is not encrypted.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Intelligible data that has meaning and can be read or acted upon without the application of decryption. Also known as cleartext.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Usable data that is formatted as input to a mode.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The input data to the authenticated encryption function that is both authenticated and encrypted.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The input to the authenticated-encryption function.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Intelligible data that has meaning and can be understood without the application of decryption.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Data that has not been encrypted; intelligible data that has meaning and can be understood without the application of decryption.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
1a. (I) A plan or course of action that is stated for a system or
organization and is intended to affect and direct the decisions
and deeds of that entity's components or members. (See: security
policy.)

1b. (O) A definite goal, course, or method of action to guide and
determine present and future decisions, that is implemented or
executed within a particular context, such as within a business
unit. [R3198]

Deprecated Abbreviation: IDOCs SHOULD NOT use "policy" as an
abbreviation of either "security policy" or "certificate policy".

Instead, to avoid misunderstanding, use a fully qualified term, at
least at the point of first usage.

Tutorial: The introduction of new technology to replace
traditional systems can result in new systems being deployed
without adequate policy definition and before the implications of
the new technology are fully understand. In some cases, it can be
difficult to establish policies for new technology before the
technology has been operationally tested and evaluated. Thus,
policy changes tend to lag behind technological changes, such that
either old policies impede the technical innovation, or the new
technology is deployed without adequate policies to govern its
use.

When new technology changes the ways that things are done, new
"procedures" must be defined to establish operational guidelines
for using the technology and achieving satisfactory results, and
new "practices" must be established for managing new systems and
monitoring results. Practices and procedures are more directly
coupled to actual systems and business operations than are
polices, which tend to be more abstract.
-  "Practices" define how a system is to be managed and what
   controls are in place to monitor the system and detect abnormal
   behavior or quality problems. Practices are established to
   ensure that a system is managed in compliance with stated
   policies. System audits are primarily concerned with whether or
   not practices are being followed. Auditors evaluate the
   controls to make sure they conform to accepted industry
   standards, and then confirm that controls are in place and that
   control measurements are being gathered. Audit trails are
   examples of control measurements that are recorded as part of
   system operations.
-  "Procedures" define how a system is operated, and relate
   closely to issues of what technology is used, who the operators
   are, and how the system is deployed physically. Procedures
   define both normal and abnormal operating circumstances.
-  For every control defined by a practice statement, there should
   be corresponding procedures to implement the control and
   provide ongoing measurement of the control parameters.
   Conversely, procedures require management practices to insure
   consistent and correct operational behavior.

definitions.source_name: Wikimedia Security Team Definitions
definitions.definition: A document which includes a concise set of requirements, rules or criteria, which influence and determine decisions and actions intended to manage key risks, usually describing "what" the requirements are, "why" they are required, and by "whom" they are to be implemented.

definitions.source_name: California SAM 5300 Technical Definitions (CDT)
definitions.definition: A high-level directive that describes mandatory or prohibited actions, applicable to individuals who fall within the scope of the policy, which aim to protect State information assets.

definitions.source_name: AWS Glossary Reference
definitions.definition: IAM : A document defining permissions that apply to a user, group, or role; the permissions in turn determine what users can do in AWS. A policy typically allows access to specific actions, and can optionally grant that the actions are allowed for specific resources , such as EC2 instances or Amazon S3 buckets . Policies can also explicitly deny access. Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling : An object that stores the information that's needed to launch or terminate instances for an Auto Scaling group. Running the policy causes instances to be launched or terminated. You can configure an alarm to invoke an Auto Scaling policy.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The set of basic principles and associated guidelines, formulated and enforced by the governing body of an organization, to direct and limit its actions in pursuit of long-term goals.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Statements, rules or assertions that specify the correct or expected behavior of an entity. For example, an authorization policy might specify the correct access control rules for a software component.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Statements, rules, or assertions that specify the correct or expected behavior of an entity. For example, an authorization policy might specify the correct access control rules for a software component.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A statement of objectives, rules, practices or regulations governing the activities of people within a certain context.

definitions.source_name: COV IT Glossary (Virginia)
definitions.definition: Are general statements of direction and purpose designed to promote the coordinated planning, practical acquisition, effective development, and efficient use of information technology resources. COV ITRM STANDARD GOV2000-01.1

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
(I) The portion of an original risk or set of risks that remains
after countermeasures have been applied. (Compare: acceptable
risk, risk analysis.)

definitions.source_name: California SAM 5300 Technical Definitions (CDT)
definitions.definition: The remaining potential risk after all information security measures are applied.

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: The likelihood and impact of a threat that remains after security controls are implemented.

definitions.source_name: NY State ITS Glossary
definitions.definition: Risk that remains after all mitigation efforts and IT security measures are applied.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Portion of risk remaining after controls/countermeasures have been applied.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Portion of risk remaining after security measures have been applied.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: the potential for the occurrence of an adverse event after adjusting for theimpact of all in-place safeguards. (See Total Risk, Acceptable Risk, and Minimum Level of Protection.)

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The remaining, potential risk after all IT security measures are applied. There is a residual risk associated with each threat.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Risk that remains after risk responses have been documented and performed.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Risk remaining after risk treatment.

definitions.source_name: COV IT Glossary (Virginia)
definitions.definition: The portion of risk that remains after security measures have been applied.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
1. (I) The process of identifying, measuring, and controlling
(i.e., mitigating) risks in information systems so as to reduce
the risks to a level commensurate with the value of the assets
protected. (See: risk analysis.)

2. (I) The process of controlling uncertain events that may affect
information system resources.

3. (O) "The total process of identifying, controlling, and
mitigating information system-related risks. It includes risk
assessment; cost-benefit analysis; and the selection,
implementation, test, and security evaluation of safeguards. This
overall system security review considers both effectiveness and
efficiency, including impact on the mission and constraints due to
policy, regulations, and laws." [SP30]

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: The process of identifying, analyzing, assessing, and communicating risk and accepting, avoiding, transferring or controlling it to an acceptable level considering associated costs and benefits of any actions taken.

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: The means of governing, communicating and making decisions about risk.

definitions.source_name: California SAM 5300 Technical Definitions (CDT)
definitions.definition: The process of managing risks to operations (including mission, functions, image, or reputation), assets, or individuals resulting from the operation of an information asset. It includes risk assessment; cost-benefit analysis; the selection, implementation, and assessment of security controls; and the formal authorization to operate the asset. The process considers effectiveness, efficiency, and constraints due to laws, directives, policies, or regulations.

definitions.source_name: NY State ITS Glossary
definitions.definition: A process that includes taking actions to assess risk and avoid or reduce risk to acceptable levels.

definitions.source_name: COV IT Glossary (Virginia)
definitions.definition: Identification and implementation of information security controls in order to reduce risks to an acceptable level. (Context: Technology Management) The process of identifying, analyzing, and responding to risk factors throughout the life of a project and in the best interests of its objectives. The art and science of identifying, analyzing, and responding to risk factors throughout the life of a project and in the best interests of its objectives.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
(D) Euphemism for non-technical or low-technology methods, often
involving trickery or fraud, that are used to attack information
systems. Example: phishing.

Deprecated Term: IDOCs SHOULD NOT use this term; it is too vague.
Instead, use a term that is specific with regard to the means of
attack, e.g., blackmail, bribery, coercion, impersonation,
intimidation, lying, or theft.

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: the use of deception to manipulate individuals into divulging confidential or personal information that may be used for fraudulent purposes

definitions.source_name: Washington State AG Data Breach Glossary
definitions.definition: The use of deception to manipulate individuals into divulging confidential or personal information that may be used for fraudulent purposes.

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: A technique an attacker uses to manipulate people into carrying out specific actions, or divulging information.

definitions.source_name: Texas DIR Cybersecurity Terminology
definitions.definition: A general term for attackers trying to trick people into revealing sensitive information or performing certain actions, such as downloading and executing files that appear to be benign but are actually malicious.

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: The practice of obtaining confidential information by manipulation of legitimate users. A social engineer will commonly use the telephone or internet to trick people into revealing sensitive information. For example, phishing is a type of social engineering.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
(I) A computer program that can run independently, can propagate a
complete working version of itself onto other hosts on a network,
and may consume system resources destructively. (See: mobile code,
Morris Worm, virus.)

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: A self-replicating, self-propagating, self-contained program that uses networking mechanisms to spread itself.

definitions.source_name: Glossário de Segurança da Informação (GSI/PR Brazil)
definitions.definition: programa capaz de se propagar automaticamente pelas redes, enviando cópias de si mesmo de computador para computador. Diferente do vírus, o worm não embute cópias de si mesmo em outros programas ou arquivos, e não necessita ser explicitamente executado para se propagar. Sua propagação se dá por meio da exploração de vulnerabilidades existentes ou de falhas na configuração de programas instalados em computadores;

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: A type of malware, similar to a virus, which self-replicates across networks without the need for a user to open an infected program.

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: A malicious program that executes independently and self-replicates, usually through network connections, to cause damage (e.g. deleting files, sending documents via email, or taking up bandwidth).

definitions.source_name: Cyber Law Toolkit Glossary
definitions.definition: A type of malware that is able to self-replicate and autonomously spread across computer networks.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
1. (I) Protection of system resources against unauthorized access.

2. (I) A process by which use of system resources is regulated
according to a security policy and is permitted only by authorized
entities (users, programs, processes, or other systems) according
to that policy. (See: access, access control service, computer
security, discretionary access control, mandatory access control,
role-based access control.)

3. (I) /formal model/ Limitations on interactions between subjects
and objects in an information system.

4. (O) "The prevention of unauthorized use of a resource,
including the prevention of use of a resource in an unauthorized
manner." [I7498-2]

5. (O) /U.S. Government/ A system using physical, electronic, or
human controls to identify or admit personnel with properly
authorized access to a SCIF.

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: The process of granting or denying specific requests for or attempts to: 1) obtain and use information and related information processing services; and 2) enter specific physical facilities.

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: A way of ensuring that only authorised users (or automated systems) can access data or services.

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: Certifying that only authorized access is given to assets (both physical and electronic). For physical assets, access control may be required for a facility or restricted area (e.g. screening visitors and materials at entry points, escorting visitors). For IT assets, access controls may be required for networks, systems, and information (e.g. restricting users on specific systems, limiting account privileges).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of granting or denying specific requests to 1) obtain and use information and related information processing services and 2) enter specific physical facilities (e.g., federal buildings, military establishments, border crossing entrances).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The decision to permit or deny a subject access to system objects (network, data, application, service, etc.)

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: To ensure that an entity can only access protected resources if they have the appropriate permissions based on the predefined access control policies.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The right or a permission that is granted to a system entity to access a system resource.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The official management decision given by a senior organizational official to authorize the operation of an information system and to explicitly accept the risk to organizational operations and assets, individuals, other organizations, and the Nation, based on the implementation of an agreed-upon set of security controls.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of granting or denying specific requests to: 1) obtain and use information and related information processing services; and 2) enter specific physical facilities (e.g., federal buildings, military establishments, border crossing entrances).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Access privileges granted to a user, program, or process or the act of granting those privileges.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of permitting or restricting access to applications at a granular level, such as per-user, per-group, and per-resources.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Procedures and controls that limit or detect access to critical information resources. This can be accomplished through software, biometrics devices, or physical access to a controlled space.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The granting or denying of access rights to a user, program, or process.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Enable authorized use of a resource while preventing unauthorized use or use in an unauthorized manner.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Process of granting access to information system resources only to authorized users, programs, processes, or other systems.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of granting access to information technology (IT) system resources only to authorized users, programs, processes, or other systems.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Restricts resource access to only privileged entities.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Restricts access to resources only to privileged entities.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of granting or denying specific requests to: (i) obtain and use information and related information processing services; and (ii) enter specific physical facilities (e.g., Federal buildings, military establishments, and border-crossing entrances).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: As used in this Recommendation, the set of procedures and/or processes that only allow access to information in accordance with pre-established policies and rules.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Restricts resource access to only authorized entities.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of granting or denying specific requests for obtaining and using information and related information processing services; and to enter specific physical facilities (e.g., Federal buildings, military establishments, and border crossing entrances).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of limiting access to resources of a system only to authorized programs, processes, or other systems (in a network).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of granting or denying specific requests for obtaining and using information and related information processing services.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of granting or denying specific requests: 1) obtain and use information and related information processing services; and 2) enter specific physical facilities (e.g., Federal buildings, military establishments, border crossing entrances).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Restricts access to resources to only privileged entities.

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: A notification that a specific attack has been detected or directed at an organization’s information systems.

definitions.source_name: California SAM 5300 Technical Definitions (CDT)
definitions.definition: Notification that a potential disaster situation is imminent, exists, or has occurred; usually includes a directive for personnel to stand by for possible activation.

definitions.source_name: Texas DIR Cybersecurity Terminology
definitions.definition: A brief, usually human-readable, technical notification regarding current vulnerabilities, exploits, and other security issues. Also known as an advisory, bulletin, or vulnerability note.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Notification that a specific attack has been directed at an organization’s information systems.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A brief, usually human-readable, technical notification regarding current vulnerabilities, exploits, and other security issues. Also known as an advisory, bulletin, or vulnerability note.

definitions.source_name: COV IT Glossary (Virginia)
definitions.definition: Notification that an event has occurred or may occur.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
(I) /noun or adjective/ Refers to alternate means of performing
system functions despite loss of system resources. (See:
contingency plan).

Example: A reserve copy of data, preferably one that is stored
separately from the original, for use if the original becomes lost
or damaged. (Compare: archive.)

definitions.source_name: Glossário de Segurança da Informação (GSI/PR Brazil)
definitions.definition: conjunto de procedimentos que permitem salvaguardar os dados de um sistema computacional, garantindo guarda, proteção e recuperação. Tem a fidelidade ao original assegurada. Esse termo também é utilizado para identificar a mídia em que a cópia é realizada;

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: A copy of computer data, usually stored in the cloud or on removable media, that can be used to recover from data loss or theft.

definitions.source_name: INCIBE Glosario de terminos de ciberseguridad
definitions.definition: Copia de seguridad que se realiza sobre ficheros o aplicaciones contenidas en un ordenador con la finalidad de recuperar los datos en el caso de que el sistema de información sufra daños o pérdidas accidentales de los datos almacenados. Los dispositivos más empleados para llevar a cabo la técnica de backup pueden ser discos duros, discos ópticos, USB o DVD. También es común la realización de copias de seguridad mediante servicios de copia basados en la nube. Es de suma importancia mantener actualizada la copia de seguridad así como tener la máxima diligencia de su resguardo, para evitar pérdidas de información que pueden llegar a ser vitales para el funcionamiento ya sea de una empresa, institución o de un contenido de tipo personal. Además, cada cierto tiempo es conveniente comprobar que la copia de seguridad puede restaurarse con garantías. Sinónimo: Copia de seguridad, copia de respaldo

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A copy of files and programs made to facilitate recovery if necessary.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A copy of files and programs made to facilitate recovery, if necessary.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Copy of files and programs made to facilitate recovery if necessary.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A copy of information to facilitate recovery during the cryptoperiod of the key, if necessary.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A copy of information to facilitate recovery, if necessary.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Duplicating data onto another medium.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A copy of key information to facilitate recovery during the cryptoperiod of the key, if necessary.

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: Blockchain technology enhances cybersecurity by replacing centralized systems with a decentralized, immutable, and cryptographically secure ledger. It reduces single points of failure, secures IoT networks, and provides transparent data integrity. Key benefits include improved authentication, tamper-proof storage, and reduced fraud, though it faces risks from 51% attacks and node-level hacking.

definitions.source_name: Glossário de Segurança da Informação (GSI/PR Brazil)
definitions.definition: base de dados que mantém um conjunto de registros que crescem continuamente. Novos registros são apenas adicionados à cadeia existente, sem que nenhum registro seja apagado;

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: A blockchain is a write-only database, dispersed over a network of interconnected computers, that uses cryptography to create a tamper-proof public record of transactions. Because blockchain technology is transparent, secure and decentralized, a central actor cannot alter the public record.

definitions.source_name: ANSSI CyberDico
definitions.definition: Véritable registre de comptes numériques reposant sur la confiance, la chaîne de blocs (blockchain), permet une gestion collaborative et sans intermédiaire (État, banque, notaire, etc.) des transactions qui s’opèrent entre différents acteurs. Cette technologie repose sur un procédé cryptographique qui assemble ces transactions pour former des « blocs » qui, une fois validés par ce même procédé, sont ajoutés à la chaîne de blocs à laquelle ont accès les utilisateurs. On compte parmi ces transactions l’échange de cybermonnaie (ou monnaie cryptographique) comme le bitcoin auquel on doit la popularisation de la Blockchain.

definitions.source_name: Krátky slovník hybridných hrozieb (Slovak NBU)
definitions.definition: Infraštruktúra, ktorá umožňuje rôznym distribuovaným softvérovým aplikáciám ukladať, uchovávať a sprístupňovať údaje (ale aj predpisy o spracovaní údajov – tzv. smart kontrakty) spôsobom, ktorý zaručuje vysokú dostupnosť a integritu týchto údajov (údaje sú autentické, nepopierateľné, nie je možné ich meniť a sú prakticky nezničiteľné) a ak je to žiadúce aj dôvernosť a vysokú mieru ochrany.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
1. (I) /general English/ A document that attests to the truth of
something or the ownership of something.

2. (I) /general security/ See: capability token, digital
certificate.

3. (I) /PKI/ See: attribute certificate, public-key certificate.

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: A form of digital identity for a computer, user or organisation to allow the authentication and secure exchange of data.

definitions.source_name: AWS Glossary Reference
definitions.definition: A credential that some AWS products use to authenticate AWS accounts and users. Also known as an X.509 certificate . The certificate is paired with a private key.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies a public key (which has a corresponding private key) and an owner that is authorized to use the key pair. The certificate contains the owner’s public key and possibly other information and is digitally signed by a Certification Authority (i.e., a trusted party), thereby binding the public key to the owner.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies a public key that has a corresponding private key and an owner that is authorized to use the key pair. The certificate contains the owner’s public key and possibly other information and is digitally signed by a certification authority (i.e., a trusted party), thereby binding the public key to the owner.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A digital representation of information which at least (1) identifies the certification authority issuing it, (2) names or identifies its subscriber, (3) contains the subscriber's public key, (4) identifies its operational period, and (5) is digitally signed by the certification authority issuing it. [ABADSG]. As used in this CP, the term “Certificate” refers to certificates that expressly reference the OID of this CP in the “Certificate Policies” field of an X.509 v.3 certificate.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A digital representation of information which at least (1) identifies the certification authority issuing it, (2) names or identifies it’s Subscriber, (3) contains the Subscriber’s public key, (4) identifies it’s operational period, and (5) is digitally signed by the certification authority issuing it.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A digital representation of information which at least (1) identifies the certification authority (CA) issuing it, (2) names or identifies its subscriber, (3) contains the subscriber’s public key, (4) identifies its operational period, and (5) is digitally signed by the certification authority issuing it.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: See certificate.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A digitally signed data structure defined in the X.509 standard [IS0 94-8] that binds the identity of a certificate holder (or subject) to a public key.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A data structure that contains an entity’s identifier(s), the entity's public key (including an indication of the associated set of domain parameters) and possibly other information, along with a signature on that data set that is generated by a trusted party, i.e. a certificate authority, thereby binding the public key to the included identifier(s).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies a key pair owner that is authorized to use the key pair, contains the owner’s public key and possibly other information, and is digitally signed by a Certification Authority (i.e., a trusted party), thereby binding the public key to the owner.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: See public-key certificate.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: See public key certificate.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A data structure that contains an entity’s identifier(s), the entity's public key (including an indication of the associated set of domain parameters) and possibly other information, along with a signature on that data set that is generated by a trusted party, i.e., a certificate authority, thereby binding the public key to the included identifier(s).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies an entity, contains the entity's public key and possibly other information, and is digitally signed by a trusted party, thereby binding the public key to the entity. Additional information in the certificate could specify how the key is used and its validity period.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies an entity, contains the entity’s public key and possibly other information, and is digitally signed by a trusted party, thereby binding the public key to the entity identified in the certificate. Additional information in the certificate could specify how the key is used and the validity period of the certificate.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies an entity, contains the entity’s public key and possibly other information, and is digitally signed by a trusted party, thereby binding the public key to the entity. Additional information in the certificate could specify how the key is used and its validity period.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies an entity, contains the entity's public key and possibly other information, and is digitally signed by a trusted party, thereby binding the public key to the entity. Additional information in the certificate could specify how the key is used and its validity period.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies an entity, contains the entity's public key and possibly other information, and is digitally signed by a trusted party, thereby binding the public key to the entity. Additional information in the certificate could specify how the key is used and its validity period. (Certificates in this practice guide are based on IETF RFC 5280).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies an entity, contains the entity's public key and possibly other information, and is digitally signed by a trusted party, thereby binding the public key to the entity. Additional information in the certificate could specify how the key is used and its validity period. (Certificates in this practice guide are based on IETF RFC 5280.)

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Also known as a digital certificate. A digital representation of information which at least 1. identifies the certification authority issuing it, 2. names or identifies its subscriber, 3. contains the subscriber's public key, 4. identifies its operational period, and 5. is digitally signed by the certification authority issuing it.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A data structure that contains an entity’s identifier(s), the entity's public key and possibly other information, along with a signature on that data set that is generated by a trusted party, i.e. a certificate authority, thereby binding the public key to the included identifier(s).

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of data that uniquely identifies an entity, contains the entity's public key and possibly other information, and is digitally signed by a trusted party, thereby binding the public key to the entity. Additional information in the certificate could specify how the key is used and its cryptoperiod.

definitions.source_name: Kubernetes Glossary
definitions.definition:
A cryptographically secure file used to validate access to the Kubernetes cluster.

Certificates enable applications within a Kubernetes cluster to access the Kubernetes API securely. Certificates validate that clients are allowed to access the API.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
1. (O) /noun/ Synonym for "cipher text" [I7498-2].

2. (I) /adjective/ Referring to cipher text. Usage: Commonly used
instead of "cipher-text". (Compare: cleartext, plaintext.)

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: Ciphertext is the unreadable, encrypted form of data (plaintext) created by applying an encryption algorithm, designed to ensure confidentiality during storage or transmission. It remains unintelligible without the correct decryption key. Ciphertext is critical for securing data at rest and in transit.

definitions.source_name: AWS Glossary Reference
definitions.definition: Information that has been encrypted , as opposed to plaintext , which is information that has not.

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: A cryptography term for encrypted information.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Data in its encrypted form.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The encrypted form of the plaintext.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Data in its encrypted form.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Encrypted (enciphered) data.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Encrypted data.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The output of the CCM encryption-generation process.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The encrypted form of the plaintext.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The confidential form of the plaintext that is the output of the authenticated-encryption function.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Data in its enciphered form.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
1. (I) /HTTP/ Data exchanged between an HTTP server and a browser
(a client of the server) to store state information on the client
side and retrieve it later for server use.

Tutorial: An HTTP server, when sending data to a client, may send
along a cookie, which the client retains after the HTTP connection
closes. A server can use this mechanism to maintain persistent
client-side state information for HTTP-based applications,
retrieving the state information in later connections. A cookie
may include a description of the range of URLs for which the state
is valid. Future requests made by the client in that range will
also send the current value of the cookie to the server. Cookies
can be used to generate profiles of web usage habits, and thus may
infringe on personal privacy.

2. (I) /IPsec/ Data objects exchanged by ISAKMP to prevent certain
denial-of-service attacks during the establishment of a security
association.

3. (D) /access control/ Synonym for "capability token" or
"ticket".

Deprecated Definition: IDOCs SHOULD NOT use this term with
definition 3; that would duplicate the meaning of better-
established terms and mix concepts in a potentially misleading
way.

definitions.source_name: INCIBE Glosario de terminos de ciberseguridad
definitions.definition: Una cookie es un pequeño fichero que almacena información enviada por un sitio web y que se almacena en el equipo del usuario, de manera que el sitio web puede consultar la actividad previa del usuario. Sus principales funciones son: - Llevar el control de usuarios: cuando un usuario introduce su nombre de usuario y contraseña, se almacena una cookie para que no tenga que estar introduciéndolas para cada página del servidor. - Recabar información sobre los hábitos de navegación del usuario. Esto puede significar una ataque contra la privacidad de los usuarios y es por lo que hay que tener cuidado con ellas.

definitions.source_name: NY State ITS Glossary
definitions.definition: A unique text file stored on a user's computer by an Internet browser. These text files are used as a means of distinguishing among users of a website and as a means of customizing the website according to the user's preferences and interests. A cookie will not include personal information unless the user has volunteered that information.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A piece of state information supplied by a web server to a browser that is temporarily stored and returned to the server on any subsequent visits or requests.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A piece of state information supplied by a Web server to a browser, in a response for a requested resource, for the browser to store temporarily and return to the server on any subsequent visits or requests.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A small file that stores information for a Web site.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A small file that stores information for a Web site on a user’s computer.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A character string, placed in a web browser’s memory, which is available to websites within the same Internet domain as the server that placed them in the web browser. Cookies are used for many purposes and may be assertions or may contain pointers to assertions. See Section 9.1.1 for more information.

definitions.source_name: ANSSI CyberDico
definitions.definition: Information stockée par le navigateur sur le disque dur lors de la consultation d'un site Internet, qui permet au serveur de mémoriser des informations sur l'internaute et son comportement.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
1. (I) The mathematical science that deals with transforming data
to render its meaning unintelligible (i.e., to hide its semantic
content), prevent its undetected alteration, or prevent its
unauthorized use. If the transformation is reversible,
cryptography also deals with restoring encrypted data to
intelligible form. (See: cryptology, steganography.)

2. (O) "The discipline which embodies principles, means, and
methods for the transformation of data in order to hide its
information content, prevent its undetected modification and/or
prevent its unauthorized use.... Cryptography determines the
methods used in encipherment and decipherment." [I7498-2]

Tutorial: Comprehensive coverage of applied cryptographic
protocols and algorithms is provided by Schneier [Schn].
Businesses and governments use cryptography to make data
incomprehensible to outsiders; to make data incomprehensible to
both outsiders and insiders, the data is sent to lawyers for a
rewrite.

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: The use of mathematical techniques to provide security services, such as confidentiality, data integrity, entity authentication, and data origin authentication.

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: Technologies primarily based on maths and computer science for protecting information so that it is only accessible to authorised recipients.

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: The study of techniques used to make plain information unreadable, as well as to convert it back to a readable form.

definitions.source_name: COV IT Glossary (Virginia)
definitions.definition: The process of transforming plain text into cipher text, and cipher text into plain text.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition: (I) See: computer security incident response team.

definitions.source_name: ENISA Glossary of Terms
definitions.definition: Computer-security and incident-response team. Computer Security and Incident Response Team. Other term for CERT.

definitions.source_name: Glossário de Segurança da Informação (GSI/PR Brazil)
definitions.definition: ) - sigla internacional para designar um grupo de resposta a incidentes de segurança, responsável por tratar incidentes de segurança para um público alvo específico;

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: Cyber security incident response team. An expert group that manages the response to a cyber security event or incident.

definitions.source_name: INCIBE Glosario de terminos de ciberseguridad
definitions.definition:
Acrónimo de Computer Security Incident Response Team, también conocido en español como equipo de respuesta a incidentes de seguridad informáticos, es el equipo encargado de recibir, comprobar y responder a incidentes que se detecten en su área de actuación. Es considerado como el equivalente en Europa de su contraparte estadounidense CERT.

#### Sinónimo: CERT

definitions.source_name: CVE Glossary
definitions.definition: The CVE trademark and the name Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures.

definitions.source_name: Glossário de Segurança da Informação (GSI/PR Brazil)
definitions.definition: sigla de common vulnerabilities and exposures;

definitions.source_name: INCIBE Glosario de terminos de ciberseguridad
definitions.definition: Acrónimo del inglés en Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures; en español, listado de vulnerabilidades de seguridad conocidas, en el que se puede identificar una vulnerabilidad, así como un resumen de las características, efectos, las versiones del software afectadas, posibles soluciones o mitigaciones de dicha vulnerabilidad.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A dictionary of common names for publicly known information system vulnerabilities.

definitions.source_name: GitLab Security Glossary
definitions.definition: Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures (CVE®) is a list of common identifiers for publicly known cybersecurity vulnerabilities. The list is managed by the Mitre Corporation .

definitions.source_name: Government of Canada Enterprise Cyber Security Strategy — Glossary
definitions.definition: Cyber security refers to the security of the transmission of electronic data and information across cyberspace. It covers the technology, processes, practices, and response and mitigation measures designed to protect electronic information, data and information infrastructure from mischief, unauthorized use or disruption in cyberspace. Cyber security complements IT security. Cyber security operationalizes the IT security controls set out in subsection B.2.3 of Appendix B of the Directive on Security Management.

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: The protection of devices, services and networks - and the information on them - from unauthorised access, theft or damage.

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: The protection of digital information, as well as the integrity of the infrastructure housing and transmitting digital information. More specifically, cyber security includes the body of technologies, processes, practices and response and mitigation measures designed to protect networks, computers, programs and data from attack, damage or unauthorized access so as to ensure confidentiality, integrity and availability.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The ability to protect or defend the use of cyberspace from cyber attacks.

definitions.source_name: COV IT Glossary (Virginia)
definitions.definition: Practice of defending computers, servers, mobile devices, electronic systems, networks, and data from malicious attacks. It's also known as information technology security or electronic information security.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
(I) Information in a specific representation, usually as a
sequence of symbols that have meaning.

Usage: Refers to both (a) representations that can be recognized,
processed, or produced by a computer or other type of machine, and
(b) representations that can be handled by a human.

definitions.source_name: California SAM 5300 Technical Definitions (CDT)
definitions.definition: A representation of facts, concepts, or instructions in a formalized manner suitable for communication, interpretation, or processing by humans or by automated means.

definitions.source_name: NY State ITS Glossary
definitions.definition: A subset of information in an electronic format that allows it to be retrieved or transmitted.

definitions.source_name: Cyber Law Toolkit Glossary
definitions.definition: The basic element that can be processed or produced by a computer to convey information.

definitions.source_name: COV IT Glossary (Virginia)
definitions.definition: An arrangement of numbers, characters, and/or images that represent concepts symbolically. Data refers to raw facts that include numbers, letters and symbols, images and sounds entered into a computer for processing. Data is all the raw, unprocessed facts, concepts or instructions that form the basic materials for transforming or processing into useful and meaningful results. Such facts are of little meaning or significance until they are sorted or put in a more useful form. When stored electronically in files, data can be used directly as input for the information system.

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: The unauthorized movement or disclosure of sensitive information to a party, usually outside the organization, that is not authorized to have or see the information.

definitions.source_name: Washington State AG Data Breach Glossary
definitions.definition: An incident where confidential, sensitive, or protected data is accessed, disclosed, or used by an unauthorized person or entity.

definitions.source_name: Texas DIR Cybersecurity Terminology
definitions.definition: a security incident in which sensitive, protected or confidential data is copied, transmitted, viewed, stolen or used by an individual unauthorized to do so.

definitions.source_name: Glossario CERT-AGID
COV IT Glossary (Virginia)
definitions.definition: The unauthorized access and acquisition of unredacted computerized data that compromises the security or confidentiality of personal information. Good faith acquisition of personal information by an employee or agent of an individual or entity for the purposes of the individual or entity that is authorized to view the data is not a breach of the security of the system, provided that the personal information is not used for a purpose other than a lawful purpose of the individual or entity or subject to further unauthorized disclosure.

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: a cybercrime in which the attacker floods a target with internet traffic to prevent users from accessing connected online services and sites

definitions.source_name: Glossário de Segurança da Informação (GSI/PR Brazil)
definitions.definition: sigla de negação de serviço distribuída ( distributed denial of service );

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: Distributed Denial of Service. A type of cyber attack where a computer service is overloaded by requests from multiple computers or locations, so that real users can no longer access the service.

definitions.source_name: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security Glossary
definitions.definition: See Distributed denial-of-service attack .

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A denial of service technique that uses numerous hosts to perform the attack.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition: (I) See: secondary definition under "encryption".

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: The process of transforming ciphertext into its original plaintext.

definitions.source_name: Wikimedia Security Team Definitions
definitions.definition: Decryption is the process of transforming ciphertext (information unreadable to anyone without the key) into plaintext (information readable to anyone). This is completed by using the key along with the encryption algorithm in reverse.

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: Making information that has been encrypted readable again.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of transforming ciphertext into plaintext.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of changing ciphertext into plaintext using a cryptographic algorithm and key.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of transforming ciphertext into plaintext.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of a confidentiality mode that transforms encrypted data into the original usable data.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of transforming ciphertext into plaintext using a cryptographic algorithm and key.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The process of changing ciphertext into plaintext.

definitions.source_name: RFC 4949 - Internet Security Glossary v2
definitions.definition:
1a. (I) /general security/ An environment or context that (a)
includes a set of system resources and a set of system entities
that have the right to access the resources and (b) usually is
defined by a security policy, security model, or security
architecture. (See: CA domain, domain of interpretation, security
perimeter. Compare: COI, enclave.)

Tutorial: A "controlled interface" or "guard" is required to
transfer information between network domains that operate under
different security policies.

1b. (O) /security policy/ A set of users, their information
objects, and a common security policy. [DoD6, SP33]

1c. (O) /security policy/ A system or collection of systems that
(a) belongs to a community of interest that implements a
consistent security policy and (b) is administered by a single
authority.

2. (O) /COMPUSEC/ An operating state or mode of a set of computer
hardware.

Tutorial: Most computers have at least two hardware operating
modes [Gass]:
-  "Privileged" mode: a.k.a. "executive", "master", "system",
   "kernel", or "supervisor" mode. In this mode, software can
   execute all machine instructions and access all storage
   locations.
-  "Unprivileged" mode: a.k.a. "user", "application", or "problem"
   mode. In this mode, software is restricted to a subset of the
   instructions and a subset of the storage locations.

3. (O) "A distinct scope within which certain common
characteristics are exhibited and common rules are observed."
[CORBA]

4. (O) /MISSI/ The domain of a MISSI CA is the set of MISSI users
whose certificates are signed by the CA.

5. (I) /Internet/ That part of the tree-structured name space of
the DNS that is at or below the name that specifies the domain. A
domain is a subdomain of another domain if it is contained within
that domain. For example, D.C.B.A is a subdomain of C.B.A

6. (O) /OSI/ An administrative partition of a complex distributed
OSI system.

definitions.source_name: NCSC UK Glossary
definitions.definition: A set of network addresses on the internet, managed by an organisation or person.

definitions.source_name: AWS Glossary Reference
definitions.definition: OpenSearch Service : The hardware, software, and data exposed by Amazon OpenSearch Service (OpenSearch Service) endpoints. An OpenSearch Service domain is a service wrapper around an OpenSearch cluster. An OpenSearch Service domain encapsulates the engine instances that process OpenSearch Service requests, the indexed data that you want to search, snapshots of the domain, access policies, and metadata. See also cluster . See also Elasticsearch .

definitions.source_name: NY State ITS Glossary
definitions.definition: A region of jurisdiction on the World Wide Web (Internet) for naming assignment. The General Services Administration is responsible for registrations in the dot-gov domain, and has delegated this authority to the CIO for all State Entities.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of elements, data, resources, and functions that share a commonality in combinations of (1) roles supported, (2) rules governing their use, and (3) protection needs.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Set of assets and resources subject to a common security policy.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A domain that implements a security policy and is administered by a single authority.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: An environment or context that includes a set of system resources and a set of system entities that have the right to access the resources as defined by a common security policy, security model, or security architecture. See Security Domain.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: The term domain refers to a part of the network that is administered by a single authority.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: See security domain.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A set of subjects, their information objects, and a common security policy.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: An environment or context that includes a set of system resources and a set of system entities that have the right to access the resources as defined by a common security policy, security model, or security architecture.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A distinct group of computers under a central administration or authority.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A logical structure, group or sphere of influence over which control is exercised.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A domain within which behaviors, interactions, and outcomes occur and that is defined by a governing security policy. Note: A security domain is defined by rules for users, processes, systems, and services that apply to activity within the domain and activity with similar entities in other domains.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: A domain within which behaviors, interactions, and outcomes occur and that is defined by a governing security policy. Note: A security domain is defined by rules for users, processes, systems, and services that apply to activity within the domain and activity with similar entities in other domains.

definitions.source_name: NICCS Cybersecurity Glossary (CISA)
definitions.definition: An observable occurrence in an information system or network.

definitions.source_name: AWS Glossary Reference
definitions.definition: Amazon Personalize : A user activityâsuch as a click, a purchase, or a video viewingâthat you record and upload to an Amazon Personalize Interactions dataset. You record events individually in real time or record and upload events in bulk. See also dataset . See also Interactions dataset .

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Any observable occurrence in an information system.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Any observable occurrence in a network or system.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Actions taken through the use of an information system or network that result in an actual or potentially adverse effect on an information system, network, and/or the information residing therein. See incident. See also event, security-relevant event, and intrusion.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
definitions.definition: Something that occurs within a system or network.

definitions.source_name: NIST Computer Security Resource Center (CSRC) Glossary — consolidated terminology from NIST cybersecurity publications
